---
title: "CoordCube Insertion Guide for wp.qmd"
---

## Insertion Point 1: After "Hash-less computation" section

**File:** `tagma/wp.qmd`  
**After:** Section `{#sec-paradigm-shift}` ("Hash-less computation: Direct structural addressing")  
**Insert:** The CoordCube paradigm extension

The existing section establishes that CoordPath provides structural addressing. Add a new subsection explaining that the same structural foundation also enables spatial computation:

```markdown
### Spatial interpretation: the CoordCube view

{{< include ../_include/bench/fig-bench-2ch.qmd >}}

[Insert content from `_include/snippet-coordcube-wp.qmd`]
```

---

## Insertion Point 2: After "Structural Consequences" section

**File:** `tagma/wp.qmd`  
**After:** "Structural Consequences" section (around line 315)  
**Insert:** Multi-dimensional interpretation follows from the structural design

Add a paragraph connecting the 3-axis Coord decomposition to the D×R CoordCube view:

```markdown
The same structural decoding that extracts three axes from a single Coord
extends naturally to N-Coord paths. Grouping R characters per dimension
yields a D-dimensional coordinate space where D × R = N. This CoordCube
view requires no additional decoding — the byte array is unchanged,
only its interpretation shifts from linear path to spatial coordinate.
```

---

## Insertion Point 3: "Application Domains" — expand spatial query domain

**File:** `tagma/wp.qmd`  
**Section:** "Application Domains" (around line 624)  
**Modify:** The "Graph and multi-dimensional query" paragraph

Replace or augment:

```markdown
**Graph and multi-dimensional query.** Each node maps to a coordinate,
each edge type to a bit array. Adjacency reduces to a bitwise AND over
175 machine words — no index intersection. Beyond bitwise queries,
CoordCube provides spatial region generation: proximity search,
bounding box enumeration, and distance metrics over the same coordinate
space. A graph's local neighborhood is a proximity query; a range of
timestamps and device IDs is a bounding box.
```

---

## Insertion Point 4: Appendix — add benchmark figures

**File:** `tagma/wp.qmd`  
**Section:** "Benchmarks {#sec-appendix-benches}" (around line 817)  
**Insert:** After existing benchmark figures, add the CoordCube figures

```markdown
{{< include ../_include/bench/fig-bench-cubeproximity.qmd >}}

{{< include ../_include/bench/fig-bench-cubescaling.qmd >}}

{{< include ../_include/bench/fig-bench-cubedist.qmd >}}

{{< include ../_include/bench/fig-bench-kvproximity.qmd >}}
```

Insert the following paragraph before the figures:

```markdown
### CoordCube spatial benchmarks

The following figures measure the second access channel: CoordCube-based
spatial region generation and distance metrics. All measurements are on
ARMv8.4-A Firestorm using the same benchmark harness as the core
lookup figures above.
```
